2606.13227.title: PolyAlign: Conditional Human-Distribution Alignment
2606.13227.paper_summary: Post-training methods such as supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and preference optimization typically align language models toward a single global assistant behavior. While effective for improving average helpfulness, this can suppress the natural variation of human responses across languages, tasks, and dialogue settings. We study this problem as conditional human-distribution alignment: models should match the human response distribution appropriate to the current interaction context, rather than a universal response style. We introduce PolyAlign, a distribution-aware alignment framework that organizes bilingual interaction data into bucket-specific human reference distributions defined by language, interaction track, response family, and length. PolyAlign combines Bucket-Aware SFT, which balances optimization across heterogeneous buckets, with Human-Distribution Preference Optimization (HDPO), which regularizes preference learning using critic-estimated distance to bucket-specific human support. Across a bilingual evaluation suite covering English and Chinese single- and multi-turn settings, PolyAlign improves conditional naturalness and distributional faithfulness while preserving competitive task utility. The results suggest that post-training should move beyond global alignment objectives toward interaction-aware alignment with human response distributions.
2606.13227.paper_summary_zh: 訓練後的方法，例如監督微調（SFT）和偏好優化，通常將語言模型對齊到單一的全球助手行為。雖然這對於提高平均有用性是有效的，但這可能會抑制人類在不同語言、任務和對話環境中的自然變化。我們將這個問題研究為條件人類分佈對齊：模型應該匹配與當前互動上下文相適應的人類反應分佈，而不是一種普遍的反應風格。我們介紹了PolyAlign，一個分佈感知的對齊框架，它將雙語互動數據組織成由語言、互動軌跡、反應類別和長度定義的特定於桶的人類參考分佈。PolyAlign結合了桶感知SFT，這在異質桶之間平衡優化，與人類分佈偏好優化（HDPO），這使用批評者估計的距離來正則化偏好學習，以適應特定於桶的人類支持。在涵蓋英語和中文單輪及多輪設置的雙語評估套件中，PolyAlign改善了條件自然性和分佈忠實性，同時保持競爭性的任務效用。結果表明，訓練後應該超越全球對齊目標，朝向與人類反應分佈相適應的互動感知對齊。
2606.13227.author: L. D. M. S. Sai Teja et.al.
2606.13227.authors: L. D. M. S. Sai Teja, Ufaq Khan, Sathira Silva, Xiao Wu, Muhammad Haris Khan
